reasoning:
The question asks if the RPONTSYD value will be strictly greater than its baseline (which corresponds to the latest available value for 2025-05-11, which falls on a weekend, meaning we use its Friday prior, 2025-05-09, which is exactly 0.0). Since the baseline is 0.0, an increase means the value on the resolution dates must be strictly > 0.0.

Because FRED daily series don't report on weekends, resolution dates that fall on a weekend use the previous business day's value. Examining the list:
- 2025-05-18 (Sunday) uses Friday, May 16
- 2025-06-10 is a Tuesday
- 2025-08-09 (Saturday) uses Friday, August 8
- 2025-11-07 is a Friday

Analyzing 30 recent and historic data points shows a massive day-of-week effect due to the 3-day term premium of weekend overnight repos:
- Fridays: 0 out of 6 points exceeded 0.0 (0%)
- Mondays: 2 out of 7 points exceeded 0.0 (~28%)
- Tuesdays: 5 out of 6 points exceeded 0.0 (~83%)
- Thursdays: 5 out of 7 points exceeded 0.0 (~71%)

Since three of the four resolution dates effectively resolve on a Friday, their probability of being > 0.0 is structurally quite low. The June 10 date falls on a Tuesday, giving it a much inherently higher base rate (approx. 80%).

To satisfy the constraint that uncertainty must increase (distance to 0.5 must monotonically decrease) over the forecast horizon, the probabilities are adjusted smoothly over time:
- May 18 (Friday data): Very confident it will be 0.0. Distance to 0.5 is large. -> 0.12
- Jun 10 (Tuesday data): Strong chance of > 0.0. Distance shrinks. -> 0.75
- Aug 09 (Friday data): Uncertainty increases over horizon. -> 0.31
- Nov 07 (Friday data): Highest uncertainty / closest to 0.5. -> 0.42
